articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?

articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Messi nets 2 in last home game as Argentina win
article.description: Argentina beat Venezuela 3-0 on Thursday night as Lionel Messi scored two goals in his final competitive home match in front of his country's fans in Buenos Aires.
article.linkText: Messi nets 2 in last home game as Argentina win

images.name: Messi bags a brace vs. Venezuela
images.caption: Lionel Messi scores twice as Argentina ease past Venezuela 3-0 in their 2026 World Cup qualifier.

article.story:
Argentina beat Venezuela 3-0 on Thursday night as Lionel Messi scored two goals in his final competitive home match in front of his country's fans in Buenos Aires.

Messi scored a sublime goal to kick things off, before Lautaro Martínez doubled Argentina's lead in the second half. Messi then added his second of the game to seal the World Cup qualifying win for the home side, who have already secured their spot.

The result also helped Uruguay, Colombia and Paraguay clinch their spots in the World Cup in the next-to-last round of South American qualifiers.

The Uruguayans and Colombians had 3-0 wins Thursday, and the Paraguayans had a scoreless draw to advance to the global tournament along with Argentina, Brazil and Ecuador.

Peru, who needed wins in the last two rounds to advance, are now out of contention following the loss to Uruguay.

James Rodríguez opened the scoring for Colombia in the 31st minute while John Cordoba and Juan Fernando Quintero added second-half goals to secure the win over Bolovia.

Substitution, Venezuela. Jhon Murillo replaces Eduard Bello.
